methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Kraftblock is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised €20M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-sector, same-stage private companies, supplemented by one public benchmark (LNZA). The peer set spans Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others, with a range of $120M to $5.0B.

Peer-median methodology is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the six comparable companies is $355M, with a dispersion reflecting heterogeneous business models and capital intensity within the climate-tech sector. This single-method approach limits triangulation robustness but provides a defensible anchor given comparable stage, sector, and financing context.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on $355M. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, unit economics and path to commercialization relative to comparables, and the wide dispersion in the peer cohort (80x spread between lowest and highest). A more granular assessment of Kraftblock's capital efficiency and go-to-market trajectory relative to the $300M–$500M peer band would refine this range materially.
